events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a field-service visit where the observed condition is "mixed old/new belts on multi-groove." This is a known issue in belt-driven systems - mixing old and new belts on a multi-groove sheave causes problems because the old belts are stretched/worn and sit deeper in the groove, while new belts ride higher. This causes uneven load distribution, rapid wear of new belts, vibration, and potential failure.

This is a non-compliant condition. Manufacturer guides typically specify that belts on multi-groove sheaves should be replaced as a matched set. Mixing old and new belts is defective installation/maintenance.

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: medium to high - uneven loading can cause premature failure, vibration, damage to sheaves
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded (old belts are degraded)
Confidence: high
